content.text: I'll start by inspecting the current state of the CockroachDB pods.

===STS YAML===
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - exec /cockroach/cockroach start --logtostderr=INFO --insecure --advertise-host=$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
          --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 --port=26257 --cache=25% --max-sql-memory=25% --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: POD_NAMESPACE
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.namespace
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        livenessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 3
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: http
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        name: db
        ports:
        - containerPort: 26257
          name: grpc
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 2
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1

content.text: Pods are up and awaiting `cockroach init`. Peers are now reachable ("waiting for init, will retry"), so connectivity is fine — the cluster simply hasn't been initialized. Let me initialize it.

input.command: echo "waiting for pods to become ready..."; for i in $(seq 1 24); do ready=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}' | grep -c '1/1'); echo "attempt $i: $ready/3 ready"; if [ "$ready" = "3" ]; then break; fi; sleep 5; done; echo "---"; kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods

content.text: All 3 pods are ready. Now let me verify SQL readiness and node status.

input.command: echo "===SQL TEST==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT now(); SHOW DATABASES;" 2>&1; echo "===NODE STATUS==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure 2>&1
